name: Should I hire a handyman or a general contractor for my project?
headline: Should I hire a handyman or a general contractor for my project?

mainEntity.name: Should I hire a handyman or a general contractor for my project?
mainEntity.text: Should I hire a handyman or a general contractor for my project?

acceptedAnswer.text: Deciding between a handyman and a licensed general contractor depends on the size and risk level of your project. A handyman is usually fine for small, simple tasks: fixing a fence, replacing a few tiles, patching drywall, or installing a light fixture in an existing box. These jobs are lower risk and don’t usually need permits. For bigger projects in Pacifica, Daly City, or San Bruno—like kitchen and bathroom remodels, wall removals, window and door changes, decks, and any structural, plumbing, or electrical changes—a licensed general contractor is the safer choice. They understand building codes, can pull permits, coordinate multiple trades, and carry the proper insurance. Using a handyman for major work can backfire. If the work isn’t permitted or doesn’t meet code, you may face problems when you try to sell, or you might have to pay twice to fix mistakes. There’s also more risk if someone gets hurt or something is done unsafely. Think about it this way: if the project affects the structure, weather protection, or safety systems of your home, or if its failure could cause major damage, hire a licensed contractor. If it’s mainly cosmetic and small, and you’re willing to accept some risk, a skilled handyman may be enough.
